qa_pairs.question: When did the outage at the SciNet Datacenter begin in November 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: The outage at the SciNet Datacenter began on November 7, 2024.

qa_pairs.question: When was the outage at the SciNet Datacenter scheduled to conclude in November 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: The outage at the SciNet Datacenter was scheduled to conclude on November 8, 2024.

qa_pairs.question: What was the duration of the November 7-8, 2024 outage at the SciNet Datacenter?
qa_pairs.answer: The outage lasted for one day.

qa_pairs.question: Which systems and services were affected by the November 7-8, 2024 outage at the SciNet Datacenter?
qa_pairs.answer: All systems and storage at the SciNet Datacenter were unavailable, including Niagara, Mist, HPSS, Rouge, Teach, JupyterHub, and Balam.

qa_pairs.question: What was the reason for the outage at the SciNet Datacenter on November 7-8, 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: The outage was necessary for installing new electrical equipment (UPS) as part of a systems refresh.

qa_pairs.question: What specific type of electrical equipment was installed during the November 7-8, 2024 outage?
qa_pairs.answer: New electrical equipment, specifically a UPS (Uninterruptible Power Supply), was installed.

qa_pairs.question: How did the scheduler handle jobs that could not finish before the November 7-8, 2024 shutdown?
qa_pairs.answer: The scheduler paused jobs that were unable to finish before the shutdown.

qa_pairs.question: What advice was given to users regarding job submissions before the November 7-8, 2024 maintenance at the SciNet Datacenter?
qa_pairs.answer: Users were encouraged to prioritize short jobs to utilize otherwise idle nodes prior to the maintenance.

qa_pairs.question: What were the specific hours of unavailability for the SciNet Datacenter systems on November 7-8, 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: The systems were unavailable from 7 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET.
